content.text:
Given a subgroup $H$ of a group $G$, we can partition $G$ into pieces 
called **cosets**. Each coset is a "shifted copy" of $H$, obtained 
by multiplying all elements of $H$ by a fixed element of $G$.

content.title: Left Coset
content.content:
Let $H \leq G$ and $g \in G$. The **left coset** 
of $H$ containing $g$ is
\[
 gH = \{gh : h \in H\}
\]

content.title: Right Coset
content.content:
The **right coset** of $H$ containing $g$ is
\[
 Hg = \{hg : h \in H\}
\]

content.title: Cosets in $\Z_6$
content.problem:
Let $H = \{0, 3\} \leq \Z_6$. The left cosets (using additive notation) are:
$$
 0 + H &= \{0, 3\} \\
 1 + H &= \{1, 4\} \\
 2 + H &= \{2, 5\} \\
 3 + H &= \{3, 0\} = \{0, 3\} = 0 + H
$$
There are exactly three distinct cosets: $\{0, 3\}, \{1, 4\}, \{2, 5\}$.

content.title: Coset Properties
content.content:
Let $H \leq G$ and $a, b \in G$. Then:

1. $a \in aH$

2. $aH = H$ if and only if $a \in H$

3. $aH = bH$ if and only if $a^{-1}b \in H$

4. Either $aH = bH$ or $aH \cap bH = \emptyset$

5. $|aH| = |H|$
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
**(i)** Since $e \in H$, we have $a = ae \in aH$.

**(ii)** If $aH = H$, then $a = ae \in aH = H$. Conversely, if 
$a \in H$, then $aH \subseteq H$ (since $H$ is closed) and 
$H = a^{-1}(aH) \subseteq a^{-1}H \subseteq H$, so $aH = H$.

**(iii)** $aH = bH$ iff $a^{-1}aH = a^{-1}bH$ iff $H = a^{-1}bH$ 
iff $a^{-1}b \in H$ (by (ii)).

**(iv)** Suppose $aH \cap bH \neq \emptyset$, say $ah_1 = bh_2$ 
for some $h_1, h_2 \in H$. Then $a^{-1}b = h_1h_2^{-1} \in H$, so 
$aH = bH$ by (iii).

**(v)** The map $h \mapsto ah$ is a bijection from $H$ to $aH$.

content.title: Coset Partition
content.content:
The left cosets of $H$ in $G$ form a partition of $G$: every element 
of $G$ belongs to exactly one left coset.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Every $g \in G$ belongs to $gH$ (by property (i)). If $g$ belongs to 
both $aH$ and $bH$, then $aH \cap bH \neq \emptyset$, so $aH = bH$ 
(by property (iv)). Thus each element belongs to exactly one coset.

items: Find all left cosets of $\langle 4 \rangle$ in $\Z_{20}$.

Find all right cosets of $H = \{e, (1\ 2)\}$ in $S_3$. Compare 
 with the left cosets.
Let $H = \{I, -I\}$ in $GL_2(\R)$. Describe the cosets of $H$.

Prove that the number of left cosets equals the number of right 
 cosets.

marginNotes.text:
How good and pleasant it is when God's 
people live together in unity!
